Usage: bottle decant <name>[@v] [--pane right|below] [--prompt ...] [--yolo] [--cwd PATH]

Materialize a fresh session from a bottle and resume it.

Examples:
  bottle decant auth-expert                       # resume here, in this terminal
  bottle decant auth-expert@2 --pane right        # open in a herdr split
  bottle decant auth-expert --cwd ~/work/api --prompt "continue the migration"

Pitfalls:
  - Decant resumes in the CURRENT directory (or --cwd PATH) — not where the
    bottle was created. The recorded cwd is provenance (see bottle show); a note
    prints when it differs. Stand in a tree the conversation makes sense in
    before decanting.
  - Permission mode is restored from the bottle: a session bottled in
    acceptEdits/plan/bypassPermissions decants back into that mode (resume does
    not carry the mode forward, so decant re-imposes it via --permission-mode).
    A default-mode bottle stays safe-by-default — the seeded agent prompts.
    --yolo overrides any restored mode with full bypass
    (--dangerously-skip-permissions).
  - --pane right|below needs a herdr environment (it shells out to herder-spawn).
